steps.title: Decode what x = 5 represents
steps.content: The problem states that x is the number of years after 1951. When x = 5, that's 5 years after 1951, corresponding to the year 1951 + 5 = 1956.

steps.title: Interpret P(5) ≈ 563
steps.content: The function P(x) models the population. So P(5) is the population when x = 5 (year 1956). When P(5) ≈ 563, this means the population in 1956 is approximately 563.

steps.title: Match against the choices
steps.content:
• A: Describes a difference of 5, not 563—incorrect.
  • B: States the population is approximately 563 in 1956—this directly matches our interpretation.
  • C: Talks about 5 times greater, misinterpreting the exponent—incorrect.
  • D: Describes a rate of increase, not a population value—incorrect.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
